flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the build.gradle file at project level?
flashcards.back: Configures repositories and dependencies for all modules, specifies Gradle plugin version.

flashcards.front: Where is the AndroidManifest.xml file located?
flashcards.back: In app/manifests/ directory. Declares app components, permissions, and package name.

flashcards.front: What does the res/layout directory contain?
flashcards.back: XML files defining UI layouts for activities and fragments.
Specifies included modules and root project name. Example: include ':app'

flashcards.front: Difference between androidTest and test directories?
flashcards.back: androidTest: Instrumentation tests (run on device). test: Unit tests (run on JVM).
Bitmap images, vector drawables, and other visual assets for different screen densities.
Defines Gradle version used in project, ensures consistent builds across environments.
In res/values/strings.xml. Allows localization and centralized text management.
Module-specific settings: dependencies, build types, flavors, and SDK versions.
Store launcher icons in different resolutions. Keeps them separate from other drawables.

flashcards.front: What is the java directory structure?
flashcards.back: com.example.app (main code), (test) for unit tests, (androidTest) for instrumentation tests.
Activities, services, permissions, application name, and minimum SDK version.

flashcards.front: Where are menu XML files stored?
flashcards.back: In res/menu/ directory. Defines app bar menus and contextual action modes.
App themes and reusable style attributes for UI components.
Contains Gradle wrapper files to ensure consistent build environment setup.

flashcards.front: Where are animation XML files stored?
flashcards.back: In res/anim/ directory. Defines tween animations and property animations.

flashcards.front: What is the role of rootProject.name?
flashcards.back: In settings.gradle, sets the name of the root project for Gradle builds.

flashcards.front: Where are raw asset files stored?
flashcards.back: In res/raw/ or assets/ directory. For videos, audio, or other binary files.
Color definitions used throughout the app, referenced via @color/name.
Mipmap for launcher icons (auto-scaling), drawable for other images.

flashcards.front: Where are XML font resources stored?
flashcards.back: In res/font/ directory. Contains custom font files and font families.
dependencies block in app/build.gradle. Example: implementation 'androidx.core:core-ktx'
Stores arbitrary XML files accessed via Resources.getXml() at runtime.

flashcards.front: Where are navigation graph files stored?
flashcards.back: In res/navigation/ directory. Defines app navigation paths and arguments.
Android Studio project metadata (not committed to version control).
